title: Oncostemum platyphyllum
taxonomy: Oncostemum platyphyllum belongs to the family Melastomataceae, a large and diverse group of flowering plants commonly known as the melastome family. This family is primarily found in tropical regions of the world, with the majority of its members being native to the Americas, Africa, and Asia. Oncostemum platyphyllum is a part of the genus Oncostemum, which contains a limited number of species, all of which are endemic to the tropical regions of the Pacific. The family Melastomataceae is further divided into several subfamilies, with the tribe Melastomeae being one of the largest and most diverse groups within it. Classification of Oncostemum platyphyllum can be further refined through the use of molecular phylogenetics and other advanced techniques.
